content.text:
```json
{
  "sources": [
    {
      "title": "The Beethoven Sonatas and the Creative Experience",
      "author": "Newman, William S.",
      "year": 1988,
      "type": "book",
      "publication": "W.W. Norton & Company",
      "url": "https://openlibrary.org/works/OL3521634W",
      "description": "Newman's landmark scholarly study provides detailed structural and expressive analysis of Op. 27 No. 2, examining the quasi una fantasia designation, the unconventional slow-movement-first architecture, and the persistent triplet ostinato of the first movement. Contextualizes the sonata within Beethoven's middle-period harmonic language and formal experimentation."
    },
    {
      "title": "Beethoven: The Music and the Life",
      "author": "Lockwood, Lewis",
      "year": 2003,
      "type": "book",
      "publication": "W.W. Norton & Company",
      "url": "https://openlibrary.org/works/OL5736708W",
      "description": "Lockwood offers authoritative musicological analysis of the Moonlight Sonata's first movement, addressing its C-sharp minor tonality, the hypnotic triplet figuration, the harmonic suspensions in the melody, and the movement's debt to operatic accompanied recitative. Situates the work within Beethoven's creative biography circa 1801."
    },
    {
      "title": "Beethoven: Piano Sonatas (entry: Op. 27 No. 2)",
      "author": "Cooper, Barry",
      "year": 2001,
      "type": "criticism",
      "publication": "Grove Music Online / Oxford Music Online (Oxford University Press)",
      "url": "https://www.oxfordmusiconline.com/grovemusic",
      "description": "Grove Music Online's authoritative reference entry by leading Beethoven scholar Barry Cooper covers the genesis, structure, and reception of Op. 27 No. 2, including analysis of the first movement's ternary-influenced form, the pedal-point bass, the modal coloring, and the work's posthumous nickname and cultural reception history."
    }
  ]
}
```
